teachingHint: Your child is learning the /k/ sound spelled 'ck'. When 'c' and 'k' sit together at the end of a short word, they make one sound: /k/. Help them spot the 'ck' and blend: k-i-ck → kick, d-u-ck → duck. The underlined words are for your child to read — you can help with the rest!

bonusWord.teachingTip: Tricky word! We can't sound this one out. Look at the word and say 'the'. Try to remember what it looks like.

story: The duck got a kick from a sick hen. The duck said 'No!'
